definitions: To believe someone's statement or intentions, even if there is some uncertainty or evidence to the contrary.
To assume someone is innocent or telling the truth until proven otherwise.
examples: I'll give him the benefit of the doubt and assume he just forgot.
Despite the rumors, we decided to give her the benefit of the doubt.
The judge gave the defendant the benefit of the doubt due to lack of conclusive evidence.
lemma: give someone the benefit of the doubt
source: give someone the benefit of the doubt
